content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the prompt-contract changes against `origin/master`, read the key implementation files, and ran targeted cargo tests around task-prompt contract generation/consumption. I found two substantive gaps: canonical-shape validation is too permissive after the final section, and template overrides can silently bypass the new shared contract guidance.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:252-259` stops reporting canonical-heading duplicates once all expected sections have been seen, while `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:920-923` emits `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` verbatim at top level. That means a prompt override/refinement can append a second top-level canonical section like `## Acceptance Criteria` after `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` and still pass `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()`. `create_project_from_bead_context()` and prompt-review drift checks will then accept a semantically drifted contract as valid. Tighten validation so extra top-level canonical headings after the contract block are rejected unless they are structurally escaped as section body content.
   Rationale: This breaks the stated drift-detection guarantee for the canonical prompt contract and lets malformed prompts through the new validation path.
2. `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:406-445` makes `task_prompt_contract` optional for the stage/panel templates, even though the builders always generate that block (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:109-171`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:348-359`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:79-90`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1585-1596`). Any workspace or project override that omits the placeholder still resolves successfully, so the contract guidance is silently dropped and those workflow stages no longer consume the canonical bead prompt contract. Either require the placeholder for the affected templates or inject the guidance outside the override template body so overrides cannot disable it accidentally.
   Rationale: As implemented, custom template users lose the shared contract behavior entirely, which leaves the acceptance criterion only partially satisfied.
